AddFormatLib.name: AddFormatLib
AddFormatLib.description: Add a library to the format search system option (fmtsearch)
AddFormatLib.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.libs: one or more libraries to add to the format search option

AddSASAuto.name: AddSASAuto
AddSASAuto.description: Add a fileref to the SAS autocall macro option (SASAUTOS)
AddSASAuto.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.ref: one or more filerefs to add to the option

AgeCalc.name: AgeCalc
AgeCalc.description: Calculate age based on documentation found at https://web.archive.org/web/20121212071223/http://support.sas.com/kb/24/808.html
AgeCalc.usage: Use the macro as a function within a data step or procedure, for example: age=%AgeCalc(today(), DateOfBirth) Caution:    The formula will not work for people born on Feb 29 who celebrate on Feb 28. This code handles the celebration day as Mar 01. The code will also not work where/when the Gregorian calendar was not in use. See the online documenation for futher details. Notes:      The intck function returns the number of months betweeen the DOB and the date. The day functions return a 0 or 1 if the day of the DOB is greater than the day of the date to correct for leap years. Dividing by 12 returns the number of years, and the floor function rounds down for colloquial use.
AgeCalc.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.date: date to calculate the age from
parameters.dob: date of birth of the person(s) in question

BlankFind.name: BlankFind
BlankFind.description: Generate code the find blank macro program arguments. This can be used to generate "argument checks" at the beginning of a macro program to ensure that all arguments are populated. The code cannot be used to do the check because it is best to avoid nested macros.
BlankFind.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.varlist: the list of macro arguments to generate checks for, separated by a space

BmiCalc.name: BmiCalc
BmiCalc.description: Calculate BMI based on weight in ounces and height in inches.
BmiCalc.author: Chris Swenson

CheckLog.name: CheckLog
CheckLog.description: Check log(s) for issues, including lines with (e)rror, (w)arning, (i)nvalid, or (u)ninitialized messages. Additionally, other phrases associated with issues are checked, and specific phrases are ignored. The macro can also be used with non-SAS logs, as long as the log uses the same issue keywords.
CheckLog.descriptionMore: Version 2 was when I added email capabilities and some other features.) 2011-08-08  CAS     Corrected operating system macro variable reference. 2011-08-09  CAS     Added exclusion for (e)rorrs= option and for (e)rror and (w)arning options in PROC COMPARE. 2011-08-15  CAS     Set the log exclusion messages to upper case. 2011-09-01  CAS     Added another value for the RELOG argument: E - to overwrite the log with the imported log only when issues occur. 2011-09-01  CAS     Moved section that drops tables to the first section in order to avoid confusion when tables already exist. Also revised RELOG section to only execute when the LOG data set exists. 2011-09-09  CAS     Added a new argument, SHADOW=, which forces CheckLog to make a copy of the log before importing it. This helps when another instance of SAS is still generating the log, and it would cause problems to directly import it. 2011-09-09  CAS     Added "else" to if/then section for processing messages. This should help speed up really big logs. 2011-10-07  CAS     Modified section that checks what type of log is specified to work with shadow mode (copying the file first). 2011-10-19  CAS     Moved the code to drop pre-existing tables again due to macro variable issues. This code appears to be difficult to find a place for. 2011-11-23  CAS     Modified behavior during directory mode to ignore blank files. This allows the search to continue with other files. 2012-02-24  CAS     Set SHADOW=Y by default. This should result in better processing at all times and not interfere when it really is not necessary. 2012-03-20  CAS     Fixed issues with long filenames by outputting the master without PROC PRINTTO. Additionally, incorporated the DirList macro to add subdirectories. 2012-03-22  CAS     Removed the COUNTW internal macro program, since it appears that SAS 9.1 did have the COUNTW function, so it is not necessary. 2012-03-28  CAS     Converted DM POSTMESSAGE statements to use the MessageBox API for Windows users. This should help use the CheckLog macro in Enterprise Guide. 2012-03-30  CAS     Added an exclusion for the (w)arning received when opening multiple SAS sessions without locking the user profile. 2012-04-05  CAS     Minor edits including adding labels to the output data sets, adding some documentation, and moving some local variable declarations. Removed the deletion of the Log_summary data set. 2012-05-17  CAS     Added check for a note regarding values outside of a graph axis range. 2014-07-08  CAS     Moved some pieces of code from general execution to Windows-only execution. 2014-07-08  CAS     Added PRINT=Y/N argument to print log to output window. 2014-07-09  CAS     Added reference to DIRDLM instead of hard-coded slash. 2014-07-09  CAS     Varied DM command based on OS. 2014-07-10  CAS     Added LOGDEF= option to specify default log argument in PROC PRINTTO, defautled to "log". 2015-08-05  CAS     Added check to verify log copy. 2015-08-05  CAS     Added trim when outputting log for multiple logs. 2015-08-06  CAS     Added a combined data set with all log issues during multi log review (directory). 2015-08-19  CAS     Checked whether data set exists before printing. 2016-04-11  CAS     Removed a blank argument causing issues with SYSFUNC. 2016-05-12  CAS     Added a process to add email headers when using commands for the EMAILSYS option in Unix-like environments. Tested with 'mail' and 'sendmail' commands. 2017-09-28  CAS     Added a new phrase regarding conversion based on user feedback. Thanks Benoit! YYYY-MM-DD  III     Please use this format and insert new entries above. *********************************************************************************
CheckLog.usage: The output of the macro can provide information about where to find issues in the log. When issues are identified, it is best to open the original log and program to identify the source of an issue. Otherwise, the Log data set contains all lines with the Ignore and Found variables. It is suggested that the user saves the log at about the same time as using the CheckLog macro, especially if relying on the email function for communication. The original log can then be reviewed at a later time. This can be accomplished by using a DM statement (see below) or copying the Work.log file from the work directory to a permanent directory once CheckLog has run. dm log "file 'C:\Example\Log_20100801.log' replace;" wpgm; This macro can be set to a keyboard shortcut in SAS to check the current log. It has worked most consistently on F5-F8 combinations. I suggest setting it to SHF + F6, since F6 is the key for the log window. Type the following text in the keyboard shortcut definition: gsubmit '%
CheckLog.author: Chris Swenson

All arguments are optional. If no options are specified, the macro checks the current log and outputs issues to Log_issues. Input:
parameters.log: Directory of logs or full pathname of a log. Do not use single or double quotes. Note that the names of the logs in a directory should be valid SAS names.
parameters.ext=: Extension to search for when looking for logs in a directory of log, defaulted to LOG.
parameters.subdir=: Search subdirectories using the DirList macro. This macro can only be used on Microsoft Windows. N = Do not search subdirectories (default) Y = Search subdirectories (requires DirList macro)
parameters.keyword=: A list of word or words to search for in the log text, which identify issues beyond those already identified
parameters.in this macro. This can be used to add w: SAS logs that may use additionally words to indicate issues, for example, (C)AUTION or (A)LERT. This could also be used to differentiate user-specified issues from system-generated issues. NOTE: The order of the words listed matters. The words listed first will be searched for last, and will overwrite any other words found prior. Thus, the first word should have the highest priority. Of course, the original keywords will overwrite user-specified keywords.
parameters.exclude=: A data set containing issues to exclude. Include the name of the variable after the data set name if it is not LogText (e.g., work.exclusions list). This can be used when non-issues are encountered that cannot be filtered based on a simple rule (e.g., a data set name unavoidably contains the word '(e)rror'). It is not recommended that this feature be used to circumvent code revision for easily-avoided issues. Note that data set options can be applied with this argument, so a master file with exclusions can be used and filtered. Reporting:
parameters.out=: Name of the output issues data set (default: Log_issues). Note: This is automatically set when checking a directory of logs.
parameters.print=: Determines whether or not to print out the identified issues to the output window: N = No, do not print to the output window (default) Y = Yes, print to the output window
parameters.pm=: Determines whether or not the pop-up message is used: N = No, never display a pop-up message Y = Yes, always display a pop-up message (default) E = (E)rror only pop-up message
parameters.sound=: Determines whether or not a sound is played: N = No, never play a sound (default) Y = Yes, always play a sound E = (E)rror only sound
parameters.to=: Email address(es) to send the results to. Note: Email support must be set up in SAS to use this function.
parameters.cc=: Email address(es) to carbon copy the results to. Note: CC will not work without the TO argument.
parameters.relog=: Overwrites the current log with the imported log, after the CheckLog outputs notes regarding the status of the log. Best used for testing and reviewing logs. N = Do not overwrite the current log Y = Overwrite the current log with the imported log E = (E)rror only overwrite Operational:
parameters.dirext=: When checking a directory of logs, it adds the directory and extension to the log name. N = No, do not add directory and extension Y = Yes, add directory and extension
parameters.shadow=: Make a copy of the log before checking it, useful when the program is still running and generating the log. N = No, do not make a copy of the log (default) Y = Yes, make a copy of the log
parameters.abort=: Determines whether or not the following program is aborted. This is useful to halt a long program and (a)lert the user that there are issues. N = No, do not abort program when issues occur (default) Y = Yes, abort program when issues occur (C)AUTION: This option may have undesirable results. Please use it with care and thorough testing. In version 9.1.3, it has been identified that when the abort option is specified and used in a program, then the CheckLog macro is used from a keyboard shortcut, SAS crashes. There is no known workaround and it appears to be a bug in SAS 9.1.3. MACROS: For use in macros, it is much more stable to use the following statement to end the program immediately after using the CheckLog macro: %if &ISSUES>0 %then %return;
parameters.test=: Set to 'test' (i.e., test=test) to test CheckLog without turning off the log within the macro. Additionally, the data set log_keywords is output for troubleshooting.

ColorLog.name: ColorLog
ColorLog.description: Write message to log in color, using red, green, or blue
ColorLog.descriptionMore: This macro uses one of the strangest SAS code I've ever seen.
ColorLog.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.Color: Desired color, either R (Red), G (Green), or B (Blue)
parameters.Message: Message to appear in the log in the desired color

ColumnVars.name: ColumnVars
ColumnVars.description: Set macro variables for tables from SASHELP.VColumn
ColumnVars.author: Chris Swenson

#NEXT#table#DESC#library to set columns for#NEXT#columnvar#DESC#name of macro variables for tables#NEXT#name=#DESC#filter for the column name within the table#NEXT#where=#DESC#filter criteria for SASHELP.VColumn#NEXT#test=#DESC#whether to test the macro program
parameters.columnvar: name of macro variables for tables
parameters.name=: filter for the column name within the table
parameters.test=: whether to test the macro program

CompCon.name: CompCon
CompCon.description: Compare the contents (CompCon) of two data sets that should be the same format.
CompCon.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.id=: ID to compare the contents with, either NAME or VARNUM, defaulted to VARNUM
parameters.out=: output data set name, defaulted to Contents_differ
parameters.exclude=: variables to exclude from the analysis, defaulted to INFORMAT INFORML, which usually differ greatly
parameters.expect=: Y/N whether differences are expected, which changes the output from an issue to a NOTE
parameters.max=: maximum number of records to compare, defaulted to maximum of 32,767
parameters.print=: Y/N flag to indicate whether to print the comparison, defaulted to N
parameters.test=: Y/N flag to indicate whether to test the macro

parameters.ds: one or more data sets to compress
parameters.type=: type of compression, either CHAR (character) or BINARY
parameters.lib=: library on which to process the data, defaulted to WORK

Convert.name: Convert
Convert.description: A function for converting data from numeric to character, character to numeric, date/datetime to character, date to datetime, or datetime to date.
Convert.descriptionMore: To convert date/datetime variables to character, use the N type to treat the variable as only a number. Specify the informat in the format argument.
Convert.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.format: Format of source variable for character/numeric conversion (required for types C and N)

CopyData.name: CopyData
CopyData.description: Copy data from one library to another
CopyData.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.delete=: whether to delete the data sets in the input library
parameters.type=: type of copy to perform, either with PROC DATASETS or the DATA step, which allows for compression
parameters.compress=: flag to indicate whether to compress the data sets or the type of compression to perform (CHAR, BINARY)

CopyFile.name: CopyFile
CopyFile.description: Copy a file from a source directory to a target directory
CopyFile.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.infile: filename of file to copy, not including the directory
parameters.srcdir: source directory of file to copy
parameters.tgtdir: target directory of file to copy CAUTION:    This macro overwrites the target file if it exists. Use caution!

CopyPath.name: CopyPath
CopyPath.description: Copy the path of a libname or filename to the clipboard
CopyPath.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.ref: either the libref or fileref to copy

CountW.name: CountW
CountW.description: Uses the COUNTW function in SAS 9.2+, but reverts to a different process to do the same thing in prior versions.
CountW.usage: Use as a function to assign a value to a macro variable or to use within logic. Use only where functions would be used. Limitation: The macro only uses one delimiter at a time.
CountW.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.args: Argument to pass to the COUNTW function or to count in the alternative method
parameters.dlm: Delimiter to use to separate arguments

date_sk.name: date_sk
date_sk.description: Generate date secondary keys. In other words, convert dates into numeric values that look like formatted dates, in either YYYYMM or YYYYMMDD style.
date_sk.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.type: format type, either YYMM or YYMMDD

DatePart.name: DatePart
DatePart.description: Use the DATEPART function without generating issues in the log when datetimes are missing.
DatePart.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.dt: the datetime variable to use in the datepart function
parameters.as: the output variable name, defaulted to the same name as the dt argument.
parameters.source=: Y/N indicating whether to create a label that indicates the source of the new column
parameters.comma=: Y/N indicating whether to output a final comma

DelVars.name: DelVars
DelVars.description: Delete user macro variables, borrowed from SAS online
DelVars.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.like: macro variables to delete that match the specified prefix
parameters.exclude=: macro variables to exclude from deletion, currently defaulted to my directory and library macro variables (CDIR and CLIB), which should be modified for other users

DirList.name: DirList
DirList.description: Obtain a listing of files in a directory, including subdirectories if specified. If the macro is used successively in the same job, then the report (if specified) will contain the cumulative directory listing of all directories searched. A separate output data set will be created for each DIRLIST invocation.
DirList.usage: %DIRLIST(c:/data1) %DIRLIST(c:/data1, MINDATE='01JAN04:00:00:00'dt, MAXDATE='16MAR04:23:59:59'dt) %DIRLIST(c:/data1, MINDATE='00:00:00't, MAXDATE='23:59:59't, MINSIZE=1000000) %DIRLIST(d:/data2, REPORT=Y) %DIRLIST(d:, OUT=LIBNAME.DSNAME, REPORT=N) %DIRLIST(d:/documents and settings/robett/my documents/my sas files/v8) Method:     - Use Windows pipe with file reference to execute 'dir' command to obtain directory contents - Parse pipe output as if it were a file to extract file names and other info - [optional] Select files that are within the time interval - [optional] Select files that are at least as large as MINSIZE bytes and no larger than MAXSIZE - Sort records by owner, path, filename - [optional] Create report of files per owner/path if requested - [optional] Create 1-line report of files per owner/path if requested
DirList.author: Ross Bettinger

parameters.path: Valid Windows path of directory to examine (use additional Windows pipe options on the file reference).
parameters.maxdate=: [optional] Maximum date/time of file to report.
parameters.mindate=: [optional] Minimum date/time of file to report. Note: The MAXDATE and MINDATE must be SAS date/time constants in one of the following formats:
parameters.maxsize=: [optional] Maximum size of file to report (bytes).
parameters.minsize=: [optional] Minimum size of file to report (bytes).
parameters.out=: [optional] Name of output file containing results.
parameters.report=: [optional] Flag controlling report creation, defaulted to N.
parameters.report1=: [optional] Flag controlling 1-line report creation, defaulted to N.
parameters.subdir=: [optional] Flag to include subdirectories in directory processing, defaulted to N. If set to Y then all subdirectories of PATH will be searched. Otherwise, only the path named in PATH will be searched. (Note: the process may run slower if subdirectories are included)
parameters.time=: [optional] Modify type of time reported, either C (created), A (accessed), or W (written), defaulted to C.
parameters.test=: [optional] a test file to use instead of the pipe

parameters.tables: one or more tables to drop

DstCnt.name: DstCnt
DstCnt.description: Obtain distinct count of a variable
DstCnt.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.where=: filter to apply to input data set

DupCheck.name: DupCheck
DupCheck.description: Check for duplicates within a data set using specified by variables. Additionally, split the single records from duplciates and/or suggest variables that may be involved in duplication. The suggest feature uses the DupVar macro. The macro also outputs a macro variable flag to indicate whether the table is distinct, called DISTINCT with values YES and NO.
DupCheck.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.by: known BY variables, which should identify distinct rows
parameters.split: ALL/DUP/ONE flag to indicate whether to split duplicates (DUP), non-duplicates (ONE), or both (ALL) Note: This argument accepts SINGLE, BOTH, and longer versions of the arguments above (e.g., DUPLICATES), converting these to the relevant argument above
parameters.out: prefix of the output data set, used when spliting the data set into duplicates/singles, with _dup and/or _one as a suffix
parameters.suggest: Y/N flag to indicate whether to suggest which variables may contribute to duplication, defaulted to N, for the same reason as SPLIT
parameters.expect: Y/N flag to indicate whether duplicates are expected, and when found, will not be reported as a (w)arning
parameters.sort: Y/N/D flag to indicate whether or not to sort the output (single and/or duplicate records), which should be used with caution, as it could significantly increase processing time. D indicates "dynamic", which evaluates the size of the data set, using the SMALL= argument, and sets the SORT= flag to Y or N depending on whether the input data is small or not.
parameters.small=: size of what is considered a small data set, in MB, as measured by SASHELP.VTABLE, defaulted to 194 MB. This value was set after some highly unscientific tests, so please tweak it based on your environment.
parameters.test=: Y/N flag to indicate whether to test the macro. External Macro

DupVar.name: DupVar
DupVar.description: Find variable(s) that may be involved in duplication of records
DupVar.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.by: variable(s) that should identify distinct records
parameters.distinct: Y/N to complete a distinct count of variables
parameters.test=: Y/N to indicate whether to test the macro External Macro

FreqLoop.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.out=: [optional] Name of output file containing results.

OpenProperties.name: OpenProperties
OpenProperties.description: Open the properties of the specified table
OpenProperties.usage: The OpenProperties macro can be set to a keyboard shortcut: gsubmit '%OpenProperties;' The macro will open the properties of the last table generated or the copied table. If both exist, it asks the user which to open.
OpenProperties.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.table: the table to open the properties of, defaulted to last table
parameters.style=: whether to use the MESSAGEBOX macro or the WINDOW statement supplied by the OpenProperties macro when there is no argument specified, the last table exists, and the user has copied a table name. See below for details.

OpenTable.name: OpenTable
OpenTable.usage: The OpenTable macro can be set to a keyboard shortcut: gsubmit '%OpenTable;' The macro will open the last table generated or the copied table. If both exist, it asks the user which to open.
OpenTable.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.table: one or more tables to open, defaulted to last table
parameters.style=: whether to use the MESSAGEBOX macro or the WINDOW statement supplied by the OpenTable macro when there is no argument specified, the last table exists, and the user has copied a table name. See below for details.
parameters.show=: either LABEL or NAME (default) to set what is displayed for the column header

PM.name: PM
PM.description: Post a message using a pop-up
PM.author: Chris Swenson

PrintContents.name: PrintContents
PrintContents.description: Print the contents of the last table
PrintContents.usage: The PrintContents macro can be set to a keyboard shortcut: gsubmit '%PrintContents;' The macro will print the contents of the last table generated or the copied table. If both exist, it asks the user which to print.
PrintContents.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.table: one or more tables to print the contents of, defaulted to last table
parameters.short=: Y/N as to the length of the output, where Y excludes the data set label
parameters.style=: whether to use the MESSAGEBOX macro or the WINDOW statement supplied by the PrintContents macro when there is no argument specified, the last table exists, and the user has copied a table name. See below for details.

PrintType.name: PrintType
PrintType.description: Print objects from SASHELP.VCATALG, e.g., FORMAT
PrintType.author: Chris Swenson

QA_Counts.name: QA_Counts
QA_Counts.description: Compare record counts between two data sets or numbers and output a message noting whether the comparison specified was true or false.
QA_Counts.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.operator: comparison type, using operator mnemonics: EQ = equal to GE = greater than or equal to GT = greater than LE = less than or equal to LT = less than NE = not equal

RandomSet.name: RandomSet
RandomSet.description: Obtain random data using one of two methods
RandomSet.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.ds: input data set to obtain random data from
parameters.recs: number of random records to obtain
parameters.type: run type of macro, either 1 for using PROC SORT on the input data set (good for small data sets) or 2 for only selecting the random observations from the data set (good for large data sets), defaulted to 1
parameters.out=: name of output data set, defaulted to RANDOM
parameters.multi=: multiplier applied to RAND('UNIFORM') function, defaulted to 100,000

RenameVars.name: RenameVars
RenameVars.description: Rename variables within a data set based on specifications. Please note that this macro overwrites the specified data set. A backup is created in work with "_backup" appended to the specified data set name. (W)ARNING:  This macro overwrites the specified data set in the first argument with specified variables renamed.
RenameVars.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.inds: Any data set with variables matching those in the RenameVars data set.
parameters.outds: Output data set with renamed variables. The input can be used.
parameters.filter: Text to filter the RenameVars data set.
parameters.test: Flag to indicate whether or not to test the macro. Dependency: A data set in work named "RenameVars" is required to exist. The data set should have the following variables: Source, Target, Filter. Source is the original variable name, Target is the name to be used to rename the source variable, and Filter is the criteria to match when using the macro.

Reorder.name: Reorder
Reorder.description: Reorder variables in a data set
Reorder.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.match: variable list or data set to match
parameters.type=: specifies the type of data specified in the MATCH argument, either LIST or DATASET

SaveLog.name: SaveLog
SaveLog.description: Save the SAS log and output to a directory
SaveLog.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.name: name of log and output files
parameters.dir: directory to save log and output
parameters.format=: format of optional date/time stamp (see below)

SearchCode.name: SearchCode
SearchCode.description: Search SAS code or directory of code for specified term
SearchCode.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.code: specific code or directory of code to search
parameters.term: term to search for within code
parameters.skip=: Y = Yes, skip any files that could not be imported N = No, do not skip any files (default)
parameters.pm=: Y = Yes, display the pop-up message (default) N = No, do not display the pop-up message R = Display the pop-up message only with results

Select.name: Select
Select.description: Generate a SQL select statement based on the columns in a dataset and copy the list to the OS for pasting in code elsewhere.
Select.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.prefix=: prefix to put in front of column names, used for referencing the table or an alias

SelectStat.name: SelectStat
SelectStat.description: Select a record from a group of records that has the matching statistic of a particular variable (e.g., line number, date). This process can help with selecting distinct records, even if it is necessary to run multiple times.
SelectStat.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.by: variables that identify a group of records (e.g., IDs)
parameters.var: variable to select the min or max of
parameters.stat=: the statistic to select, currently only MIN or MAX
parameters.out=: name of the output data set, defaulted to DS_STAT (e.g, Test_min where DS=Test and Stat=Min)

SetVars.name: SetVars
SetVars.description: Create one or more macro variables from the variable names in a data set. The generated macro variable can either be a list within one macro variable or multiple macro variables named with the specified prefix and appended observation number.
SetVars.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.type=: either MULTI for multiple macro variables with a number appended or LIST for for one macro variable that contains a list of values
parameters.vtype=: variable type to set as macro variables, either C for character or N for numeric
parameters.sepby=: identifies the separator between items (see below)
parameters.where=: additional filter criteria for a column
parameters.sortby=: specifies how to sort the variables, either by variable order (O) (default) or variable name (N).

SortComp.name: SortComp
SortComp.description: Sort and compare two data sets
SortComp.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.id: ID to compare the data sets on
parameters.out=: output data set name, defaulted to Comparison
parameters.exclude=: variables to exclude from the comparison
parameters.dup=: Y/N whether to check for duplicates
parameters.max=: maximum number of comparisons, defaulted to 32,767
parameters.print=: Y/N whether to print the results
parameters.test=: Y/N whether to test the macro
parameters.OUT_differ: output from the COMPARE procedure for only records in common
parameters.OUT_in_1: output from the COMPARE procedure for only records in the BASE data set
parameters.OUT_in_2: output from the COMPARE procedure for only records in the COMPARE data set * where OUT is the value of the OUT= argument

TableInfo.name: TableInfo
TableInfo.usage: See the SAS documentation on the ATTRC and ATTRN functions for valid values for the infotype argument
TableInfo.author: Chris Swenson

TableVars.name: TableVars
TableVars.description: Set macro variables for tables from SASHELP.VTable
TableVars.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.tablevar: name of macro variables for tables
parameters.name=: filter for the table name within the library
parameters.test=: whether to test the macro program

TeaTimer.name: TeaTimer
TeaTimer.description: Time tea brewing by putting SAS to sleep. Useful when the user cannot install software.
TeaTimer.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.style=: either MessageBox (M) to use the MessageBox API or Window (W) to use a %WINDOW statement
parameters.sound=: flag to indicate whether to output sound with notification

TextExport.name: TextExport
TextExport.description: Export data set to tab-delim text file
TextExport.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.dir: directory to export data set to

TextID.name: TextID
TextID.description: Convert a text field to an ID that will be the same when re-run. The macro converts each part of the field into its binary representation and then sums up each character.
TextID.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.var: variable to assign an ID to
parameters.id: ID variable name, defaulted to ID
parameters.out=: output data set, defaulted to the input data set if blank
parameters.test=: Y/N flag to indicate whether to run the macro in test mode

parameters.period: Period, either B (Begin), M (Midpoint), or E (End)
parameters.note: Note to output with period time
parameters.log=: Y/N flag to indicate whether to maintain a log of the periods and messages
parameters.out=: name of log output data set, defaults to Log_time
parameters.diff=: Y/N flag to indicate whether to output a macro variable, named diff_period, containing the differences between periods

TransMerge.name: TransMerge
TransMerge.description: Transpose and re-merge all columns in a data set. The output column names will be the original followed by the ID variable, then any specified suffix.
TransMerge.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.id: ID to use in naming the output columns
parameters.idloc=: location of the ID in the output column name, either PREFIX, SUFFIX, or NONE
parameters.copy=: columns to copy and not transpose
parameters.out=: output data set name (optional), defaults to input
parameters.chardef=: character default value, defaulted to null
parameters.test=: Y/N flag to indicate whether to test the macro

ValidArg.name: ValidArg
ValidArg.description: Write code to validate macro program arguments, including the following checks (see below). The code is copied to the clipboard for the user to paste within the macro code. This macro should not be referenced within a macro, since by itself it does not complete the check, but generates code that does so.
ValidArg.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.varlist: The list of macro arguments to generate checks for, separated by spaces. Note that the specified validation types will be generated for all specified arguments.
parameters.type=: The type of check to generate, including the following:
parameters.#DESC#YN = Checks that the argument is e: YN = Checks that the argument is either Y or N
parameters.#DESC#LIST = Checks that the argument is: LIST = Checks that the argument is one of the values in the LIST argument
parameters.#DESC#NUM = Checks that all values are n: NUM = Checks that all values are numbers
parameters.#DESC#CHAR = Checks that all values are: CHAR = Checks that all values are character
parameters.#DESC#EXIST = Checks that the specified: EXIST = Checks that the specified object exists and requires the OBJ= argument
parameters.list=: Valid values for the LIST type check, separated by spaces
parameters.obj=: Type of object to check for the existence of, including:
parameters.#DESC#DATA = Data set in a library#NEXT#: DATA = Data set in a library
parameters.#DESC#DSVW = Data set or view in a libra: DSVW = Data set or view in a library

parameters.ds: input data set to count populated variables
parameters.out: output data set, defaults to ds_counts (without library)
parameters.sort: the variable to sort the final output by, either ORDER (variable/column order), NAME (variable/column name), COUNT, or PERCENT

VarInfo.name: VarInfo
VarInfo.usage: See the SAS documentation on the ATTRC and ATTRN functions for valid values for the infotype argument
VarInfo.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.ds: data set of variable in question

VarMac.name: VarMac
VarMac.description: Create macro variables from two columns in a data set
VarMac.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.namevar: variable containing the macro variable names
parameters.valuevar: variable containing the macro variable values
parameters.where=: filter criteria for input data set

parameters.library: library that the format is in
parameters.keep=: variables to keep, defaulted to START, END, LABEL, and TYPE
parameters.open=: Y/N flag to indicate whether to open the table at the end, defaulted to Y

ViewLib.name: ViewLib
ViewLib.description: Create a library that consists of views of the source library. This is useful for the 'view' library to act as a reference for the source library. The target= option can be used to rename the data sets in the source library. For example, if a library has 10,000 tables and 4,000 start with 'ZC_', you can set up a 'ZC' view library, removing the 'ZC_' from the names: %ViewLib( Clarity, ZC, where=substr(memname, 1, 3)='ZC_', target=substr(memname, 4, length(memname)-3) );
ViewLib.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.target=: function to modify the target name

XDLM.name: XDLM
XDLM.description: Import a file without delimiting the file
XDLM.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.file: directory and filename of the file to import
parameters.type=: type of filename, defaulted to blank for normal files
parameters.case=: whether to set the text of the file to upper case (U) or lower case (L)
parameters.strip=: Y/N whether to strip the text of leading and trailing blanks, defaulted to N
parameters.linelen=: the length of the Line variable, which stores the observation number
parameters.textlen=: the length of the $varying format, defaulted to 1000

ZeroCheck.name: ZeroCheck
ZeroCheck.description: Write (e)rror or (w)arning if there are records in a data set
ZeroCheck.author: Chris Swenson

parameters.ds: input data set that should be empty
parameters.msg: message to output if the data set is not empty
parameters.type: type of issue to output: (e)rror or (w)arning
